content.text: Now I'll create all the necessary resources: ConfigMap, headless Service, client Service, PDB, and the StatefulSet.

input.command:
cat <<'EOF' | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: es-config
  namespace: elasticsearch
data:
  elasticsearch.yml: |
    cluster.name: es-cluster
    network.host: 0.0.0.0
    discovery.seed_hosts: es-headless
    cluster.initial_master_nodes: es-0,es-1,es-2

    xpack.security.enabled: true
    xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: true
    xpack.security.http.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: true
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.verification_mode: none
EOF

  namespace: elasticsearch

  clusterIP: None
  selector:

  ports:
  - name: transport
    port: 9300
  - name: http
    port: 9200
---

kind: Service
metadata:
  name: es-http
  namespace: elasticsearch
  labels:
    app: elasticsearch
spec:
  selector:
    app: elasticsearch
  ports:
  - name: https
    port: 9200
    targetPort: 9200
EOF

input.command:
cat <<'EOF' | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: es-pdb
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  minAvailable: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: elasticsearch
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: es
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  serviceName: es-headless
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: elasticsearch
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: elasticsearch
    spec:
      initContainers:
      - name: fix-permissions
        image: busybox
        command: ["sh", "-c", "chown -R 1000:1000 /usr/share/elasticsearch/data"]
        volumeMounts:
        - name: data
          mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
      - name: increase-vm-max-map
        image: busybox
        securityContext:
          privileged: true
        command: ["sysctl", "-w", "vm.max_map_count=262144"]
      containers:
      - name: elasticsearch
        image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1
        ports:
        - containerPort: 9200
          name: http
        - containerPort: 9300
          name: transport
        env:
        - name: POD_NAME
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              fieldPath: metadata.name
        - name: ELASTIC_PASSWORD
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: elastic-password
              key: password
        - name: ES_JAVA_OPTS
          value: "-Xms512m -Xmx512m"
        - name: node.name
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              fieldPath: metadata.name
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "1Gi"
            cpu: "500m"
          limits:
            memory: "2Gi"
            cpu: "1000m"
        volumeMounts:
        - name: data
          mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
        - name: config
          mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml
          subPath: elasticsearch.yml
        - name: certs
          mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs
          readOnly: true
        readinessProbe:
          exec:
            command:
            - sh
            - -c
            - "curl -sk -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD} https://localhost:9200/_cluster/health | grep -vE '\"status\":\"red\"'"
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 10
          timeoutSeconds: 5
          failureThreshold: 6
      volumes:
      - name: config
        configMap:
          name: es-config
      - name: certs
        secret:
          secretName: es-http-tls
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: data
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 5Gi
EOF

DNS is now resolving `es-headless` to `10.244.0.84` (the es-0 pod). The `publishNotReadyAddresses` change is working. But the Elasticsearch still says it can't resolve `es-headless`. This might be because the JVM DNS cache is caching the negative result.

I'm realizing there's a circular dependency here: the StatefulSet creates pods sequentially, so es-1 and es-2 won't even be spawned until es-0 becomes Ready. But es-0 can't reach Ready status without forming a cluster with a master, which requires at least 3 nodes. I need to find a way to break this deadlock.

The fix is to switch the StatefulSet to parallel pod management so all three pods start simultaneously, allowing them to discover each other and elect a master without waiting for sequential readiness checks.

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: es
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  serviceName: es-headless
  replicas: 3
  podManagementPolicy: Parallel
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: elasticsearch
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: elasticsearch
    spec:
      initContainers:
      - name: fix-permissions
        image: busybox
        command: ["sh", "-c", "chown -R 1000:1000 /usr/share/elasticsearch/data"]
        volumeMounts:
        - name: data
          mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
      - name: increase-vm-max-map
        image: busybox
        securityContext:
          privileged: true
        command: ["sysctl", "-w", "vm.max_map_count=262144"]
      containers:
      - name: elasticsearch
        image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1
        ports:
        - containerPort: 9200
          name: http
        - containerPort: 9300
          name: transport
        env:
        - name: ELASTIC_PASSWORD
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: elastic-password
              key: password
        - name: ES_JAVA_OPTS
          value: "-Xms512m -Xmx512m"
        - name: node.name
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              fieldPath: metadata.name
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "1Gi"
            cpu: "500m"
          limits:
            memory: "2Gi"
            cpu: "1000m"
        volumeMounts:
        - name: data
          mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
        - name: config
          mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml
          subPath: elasticsearch.yml
        - name: certs
          mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs
          readOnly: true
        readinessProbe:
          exec:
            command:
            - sh
            - -c
            - "curl -sk -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD} https://localhost:9200/_cluster/health | grep -vE '\"status\":\"red\"'"
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 10
          timeoutSeconds: 5
          failureThreshold: 6
      volumes:
      - name: config
        configMap:
          name: es-config
      - name: certs
        secret:
          secretName: es-http-tls
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: data
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 5Gi
EOF

Hmm, es-0 is Terminating. That seems odd. Wait - looks like the rollout status command finished (which was running in the background) and deleted es-0 or something? Actually, the `rollout status` was running in the background from before, but it failed. 

Actually, I think the orphaned es-0 pod (from the old StatefulSet that I deleted with `--cascade=orphan`) is now being cleaned up when the new StatefulSet took over management. Let me check.

Actually, when I deleted the StatefulSet with `--cascade=orphan`, the pod `es-0` was left running. 

When I recreated the StatefulSet with the same name, it probably didn't adopt the orphaned pod properly. Instead, the new StatefulSet with its Parallel policy might have created its own es-0 simultaneously, leaving the old one orphaned and now terminating. So I'll wait for es-0 to finish terminating and come back up as a fresh pod under the new StatefulSet.

content.text: The cluster formed — all 3 nodes went Ready. The old orphaned es-0 pod is being replaced by the new StatefulSet. Let me wait for it to come back.
